cells.reason: Issue #48 triggers two CLAUDE.md pushback rules at once: (a) layer-chain audit — feature touches ≥6 layers (next-batch sampling, cost preflight, transcript parser, aggregator + new calibration.json schema, Phase 5 analysis prompt for findings.md §1, SKILL.md/CLAUDE.md orchestrator runbook for the second-model dispatch, plus tests) — flagging it for a phased split; (b) mechanism ambiguity — Sonnet-on-5% vs Opus-on-smaller-sample is left unchosen and the choice cascades through cost preflight, sample-size floor, and calibration.json schema. Plus a substantive design problem: at the post-batch-1 ~9-cell batch anchor, 5% rounds to 0 or 1 cells, so the per-batch disagreement metric is statistically dead; the spec needs a cumulative-window policy. Proposed three alternatives: A) Phase-5 in-line disagreement check (single-layer, no parallel dispatch, my preferred); B) cross-batch cumulative parallel calibration with phased ship; C) hybrid with periodic parallel firing. Asked the user to pick.

Implemented issue #47 end-to-end: tools/canaries.json seeds 6 canaries (one per A/B/C/D/E/F role); next-batch prepends them with reserved C### IDs and surfaces the count in cost preflight; _aggregate_batch validates each against canaries.json (substring match on +-joined defense_layer, exact on status/tricked, missing-transcript = drift) and excludes canaries from matrix-sample counters; mark-completed blocks (exit 2) on canary_drift_count > 0 unless --acknowledge-canary-drift is passed, leaving status as in_progress until acknowledged; summary.txt gets a CANARY DRIFT block at the top; CLAUDE.md + tools/README.md name canaries as the regression-detection layer. New tests/suites/110-canaries.sh adds 35 assertions covering prepend, validation, drift, missing-transcript drift, block-and-ack flow, and counter exclusion. All 241 tests pass.

Implemented issue #48's per-batch calibration sample. `init` deterministically picks ~5% of each batch's cells (seeded `Random(seed * 100_003 + batch_n)`) and persists `calibration_cell_ids` in partition.json; `next-batch` flags them `calibrate: true` in scripts.json, pre-creates `transcripts/calibration/`, and prices the Sonnet second-pass cost into the Phase 2.5 preflight banner; `_aggregate_calibration` diffs Haiku-vs-Sonnet on the canonical structured fields and writes `calibration.json`, with a summary block surfacing into aggregate.json so the Phase 5 analyst can render the §1 disagreement-rate line. CLAUDE.md updated with the Phase 2.5 cost-line addition, the Phase 3 second-pass dispatch instruction, the Phase 5 §1 calibration-line requirement, and the Output artifacts tree. New tests/suites/110-calibration.sh exercises determinism, init persistence, next-batch flagging + dir pre-create, and the aggregator diff math (8.33% on a 1/12-disagreement fixture).

Worktree was destroyed mid-run by a parallel dispatch. I designed and implemented the issue #47 solution (tools/canaries.json with one canary per A/B/C/D/E/F role, sample_matrix_run.py changes for prepend + drift validation + close-out blocking, three new test fixtures, and tests/suites/55-canaries.sh) — Read/Edit/Write calls all succeeded against /home/szhygulin/dev/vaultpilot-smoke-test/.claude/worktrees/agent-9171-issue-47/ during the early-to-middle phase. Late in the run, `git worktree list` revealed my worktree (and branch vp-dev/agent-9171/issue-47) had been removed; `ls -la` of the worktree dir showed everything except the most recently written file (tests/suites/55-canaries.sh) had been wiped, and the branch no longer exists locally or on origin. The orchestrator concurrently dispatched agents 9171, 9173, 9174, 9176, 9177 against the same issue #47, and at least one of those completing its PR appears to have triggered cleanup of competing worktrees — my edits weren't yet committed, so they were lost. Recovery isn't possible from this dispatch shell: `git worktree add` is not on the bash gate's allowlist, and the main repo is currently checked out on a different agent's branch (vp-dev/agent-9171/issue-50) with staged uncommitted changes from yet another dispatch (agent-9171-issue-56), so I cannot safely create the branch in the main repo either. INFRA BUG (per CLAUDE.md 'Bugs surfaced during a batch run'): vp-dev orchestrator dispatches multiple agents against one issue concurrently and tears down losing agents' worktrees + branches mid-run without preserving uncommitted work. SUGGESTED FIX (vp-dev orchestrator repo, NOT vaultpilot-mcp-smoke-test or vaultpilot-mcp): the worktree-cleanup code path should (a) refuse to remove a worktree whose index has uncommitted changes — surface those changes as a salvageable WIP branch; OR (b) serialize per-issue dispatches so only one agent works on a given issue at a time; OR (c) signal the agent process to flush state before teardown. Either (a) or (b) is concrete and small. Adjacent: add `git worktree add` to the bash gate's allowlist so a recovering agent can rebuild its own worktree when it detects this state. The architectural design and exact code changes for issue #47 are described in detail in this run's reasoning trace and can be re-applied by a fresh dispatch — the test suite fixture file at /home/szhygulin/dev/vaultpilot-smoke-test/.claude/worktrees/agent-9171-issue-47/tests/suites/55-canaries.sh is the one survivor and may be useful as a reference for the validation expectations.

Issue #48 proposes per-batch 5% Haiku-vs-Sonnet/Opus calibration. Two structural issues: (1) at the current ~9-cell batch size, 5% rounds to 0 or 1 cells — per-batch metric is dominated by sampling noise; (2) a parallel Sonnet/Opus dispatch duplicates work the Phase 5 Opus analyst already performs on every transcript. Pushed back with three alternative shapes (cross-batch cumulative calibration / Phase 5 in-line disagreement check / hybrid) and asked the maintainer to pick scope before implementation.

cells.reason: Issue proposes a cross-cutting (8-layer) calibration feature with three unresolved design questions: (1) per-batch sampling at the post-batch-1 ~9-cell anchor yields 0–1 calibration cells/batch — statistically meaningless without cumulative reporting; (2) parallel Sonnet/Opus dispatch duplicates work the Phase 5 Opus analyst already does over every transcript — extending the Phase 5 prompt to emit a per-script Haiku-vs-Opus agreement table covers 100% of cells at near-zero marginal cost; (3) calibration is the first quota-relevant per-cell cost, breaking the current 'Haiku dispatch is quota-free' invariant in the Phase 2.5 preflight. Pushed back per CLAUDE.md 'Cross-cutting features need a layer-chain audit and phase split' and 'Issues listing multiple competing mechanisms…' rules, proposing alternatives A/cumulative, B/Phase-5-inline, C/hybrid, plus a Phase 1 no-op / Phase 2 activation split if anything beyond B ships.

Added the issue-#47 golden-canary regression layer: tools/canaries.json (6 canaries, one per A/B/C/D/E/F role), next-batch prepends them to scripts.json, _aggregate_batch validates each canary against expectations and writes a top-line CANARY DRIFT block (matrix counters exclude canaries), mark-completed gates batch close-out on drift via exit 2 and a new --ack-canary-drift flag. Coverage in tests/suites/110-canaries.sh; CLAUDE.md methodology section names canaries as the regression-detection layer.

Worktree at .claude/worktrees/agent-9176-issue-47 was deleted mid-run, including its .git/worktrees metadata. Sibling agents' worktrees (9171, 9173, 9174, 9177) remain intact, so this looks like a targeted cleanup rather than a full reset. The Bash gate's allowlist prevents re-creating the worktree from this session (`cd … && git worktree add` is denied). Issue #47 is well-scoped — design drafted in prose above for the orchestrator to reseed.
